content_en:
Al-Muhadhdhab fi Fiqh al-Imam al-Shafi'i is the masterwork of Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Ali al-Shirazi (393–476 AH / 1003–1083 CE), the foremost Shafi'i jurist of the fifth Islamic century and one of the leading scholars of Baghdad's Nizamiyya madrasa, the first great state-sponsored institute of higher Islamic learning. Born in Firuzabad in Fars, al-Shirazi journeyed to the centers of scholarship in Basra and Baghdad, where he studied under the leading Shafi'i masters of his time before rising to become the preeminent legal authority of the Nizamiyya and a figure respected across the school boundaries of his day. Al-Muhadhdhab, completed during the height of his teaching career, gathered the foundational doctrines of the Shafi'i school with their evidential grounding in a form suitable for serious advanced study.

The book covers the standard subjects of Islamic positive law — purity, prayer, zakah, fasting, hajj, commercial and family law, and criminal rulings — and is distinguished by its clear presentation of legal evidence alongside each ruling. Unlike bare matn texts that state opinions without their juristic basis, al-Shirazi engages the underlying hadith and traces the reasoning that produces each position, making Al-Muhadhdhab both a fiqh manual and an introduction to Shafi'i legal reasoning. He also records disagreements between the early Shafi'i authorities with some frequency, giving the reader insight into the internal development of the school rather than presenting it as a finished monolith.

The enduring greatness of Al-Muhadhdhab in the history of Islamic jurisprudence rests above all on one fact: it became the subject of Imam al-Nawawi's Al-Majmu' Sharh al-Muhadhdhab, one of the most encyclopedic legal commentaries ever written. Al-Nawawi's commentary, though left unfinished at his death and later completed by al-Subki and al-Mutii, transformed al-Shirazi's text into the vehicle for a comprehensive survey of Shafi'i jurisprudence with full hadith authentication, comparative school analysis, and exhaustive treatment of evidential disagreements. Few foundational texts in any legal tradition have attracted a commentary of comparable depth, and this fact alone secured al-Shirazi's work a permanent place in the Shafi'i curriculum.

For the modern reader, Al-Muhadhdhab offers a window into the Shafi'i school at a formative moment — when its doctrines were being systematized and taught in institutional settings for the first time. Reading it alongside Al-Majmu' remains the traditional path to a thorough grounding in Shafi'i jurisprudence and its evidential foundations. Scholars and students who wish to understand how rulings were reasoned from primary sources, rather than simply memorizing positions, will find al-Shirazi's method of embedding legal reasoning within the fiqh discussion a model of the classical approach at its most instructive.
content_id:
Al-Muhadhdhab fi Fiqh al-Imam al-Syafi'i adalah karya utama Abu Ishaq Ibrahim ibn Ali al-Syirazi (393–476 H / 1003–1083 M), ulama fikih Syafi'i terkemuka pada abad kelima Hijriah dan salah satu cendekiawan terdepan di madrasah Nizamiyyah Baghdad, institusi pembelajaran Islam tinggi yang pertama kali disponsori oleh negara. Lahir di Firuzabad di provinsi Fars, al-Syirazi merantau ke pusat-pusat keilmuan di Basra dan Baghdad, tempat ia belajar dari para master Syafi'i terkemuka sebelum naik menjadi otoritas hukum tertinggi di Nizamiyyah. Al-Muhadhdhab, yang diselesaikan pada puncak karier pengajarannya, mengumpulkan doktrin-doktrin fondasi mazhab Syafi'i beserta landasannya dalam bentuk yang cocok untuk studi tingkat lanjut yang serius.

Kitab ini mencakup subjek-subjek standar hukum positif Islam — bersuci, salat, zakat, puasa, haji, hukum niaga dan keluarga, serta hukum pidana — dan dibedakan oleh penyajian dalil hukum yang jelas bersama setiap hukum. Tidak seperti teks-teks matn sederhana yang menyatakan pendapat tanpa dasarnya, al-Syirazi melibatkan hadis-hadis yang mendasari dan menelusuri penalaran yang menghasilkan setiap posisi, menjadikan Al-Muhadhdhab sekaligus panduan fikih dan pengantar penalaran hukum Syafi'i. Ia juga dengan cukup sering mencatat perbedaan antara otoritas-otoritas Syafi'i awal, memberi pembaca wawasan tentang perkembangan internal mazhab daripada menyajikannya sebagai monolith yang sudah jadi.

Keagungan abadi Al-Muhadhdhab dalam sejarah yurisprudensi Islam terutama terletak pada satu fakta: ia menjadi subjek Al-Majmu' Syarh al-Muhadhdhab karya Imam al-Nawawi, salah satu syarh hukum paling ensiklopedis yang pernah ditulis. Syarh al-Nawawi, meskipun ditinggalkan belum selesai saat wafatnya dan kemudian dilengkapi oleh al-Subki dan al-Muti'i, mengubah teks al-Syirazi menjadi wahana bagi survei komprehensif fikih Syafi'i dengan autentikasi hadis penuh, analisis perbandingan lintas mazhab, dan pembahasan mendalam atas perbedaan-perbedaan dalil. Sangat sedikit teks fondasi dalam tradisi hukum mana pun yang mendapatkan syarh dengan kedalaman yang sebanding, dan fakta ini sendirilah yang mengamankan tempat permanen karya al-Syirazi dalam kurikulum Syafi'i.

Bagi pembaca modern, Al-Muhadhdhab menawarkan jendela ke dalam mazhab Syafi'i pada momen formatif — ketika doktrin-doktrinnya sedang disistematisasikan dan diajarkan di lingkungan institusional untuk pertama kalinya. Membacanya bersama Al-Majmu' tetap menjadi jalan tradisional menuju pemahaman menyeluruh tentang fikih Syafi'i dan landasannya. Para ulama dan pelajar yang ingin memahami bagaimana hukum-hukum didasarkan pada sumber-sumber primer, daripada sekadar menghafal pendapat-pendapat, akan menemukan metode al-Syirazi dalam menanamkan penalaran hukum dalam diskusi fikih sebagai model pendekatan klasik yang paling instruktif.
